I have really tried. I started out with a review of a 3 because the axles of the stick had huge slop areas when I got it, and resulted in about an initial 40% dead-zone allocation to prevent any craft from spazzing out and said that it was adjustable but that was just about all it had going for it.

Let me explain what kind of horrible mistake this company made by selling this pile of crap right quick before I move on. The joystick was a test buy. I'd had a lot of friends that mulled back and forth between "Mad Catz" is better and "Razer" is better. So I figured, before I go all out, I'd buy something from MadCatz and something from Razer. So I got the MadCatz FLY Stick 5, and the Razer Orbweaver gaming pad. I was so horrified at the quality of the joystick, I bough a full set of Razer products and then hated the company for not offering joysticks to replace this pile of crap. When I say I bought the full set, we're talking deathstalker ultimate, oraborus mouse, headset, Vespula, the works -- in total, ole MadCatz gained $60.00, like $40.00 profit, and ole Razer? well they got a nice little $720.00 sum, and likely profited $250 of that. And there in lies Mad Catz grave error.

A few months back, I lowered that rating to a 2. The #2 button popped off and the deadzones increased to around 60%. I'm just fed the hell up at this point as well and order a Thrustmaster HOTAS to replace this steaming pile of crap. And then I have the company send me a PM wanting to know if I would want a replacement (an amazon message sent some 5 months ago) and was SOOO tempted to send them a video, in reply, of me smashing this joystick on a brick with the subject, "No, I don't want another steaming pile of crap". You see, I'm not going to waste my time to simply have them replace this crap with more crap. It makes no difference how many times it is replaced, THIS JOYSTICK IS UTTER, STEAMING, CRAP. I don't ever want another joystick like this. The point of a video game is to provide entertainment. Trust me when I tell you, it is not possible with this joystick. Its not a joystick, its a trollstick.

But I'll continue to explain why. You see, if button 2 falling off wasn't bad enough, the worst part about it was repairing it with some plastic epoxy, I was able to restore functionality to that button. But here is the kicker and that bad part, the button felt a LOT more solid AFTER I fixed it than out of the box. Imagine that, a jimmy rigged fix is more of a solid construction than the original manufactured item. You can't make this crap up! Ok, so then the other button (the one on the other side of the 2) it comes off, I fix that one as well. And, you got it, it works better and feels more sturdy.

*sigh*

So, the last straw was the trim switch you hear folks talking about. I used that in Elite Dangerous as a weapon group cycling "button". However, you would "click" down on the button once, but the joystick may register 0 clicks, or hell, it might even register 20. Like a box of chocolates, you NEVER knew wtf you would get with that button. This prompted me to coin a new word, just for this joystick, "Click and Pray".

Before ditching this Joystick I put a grand total of about 17 gaming hours on this Joystick and its not like I was as pissed as I am recounting the stress this Joystick put me through while playing Elite Dangerous, and I wasn't even PVPing, this joystick just seems to melt in your hand, sorry for the chocolate references. But you know, you don't expect Chocolate to last very long, a joystick for $60.00, no, it better give me a little more than a year or more than a $3.00 mouse.

But what gets this product a rating of 1 today is the sheer comparison of a CHEAPER joystick with included (but detatchable throttle console), has better construction quality, better materials, more buttons, and more precision on its sensors than the con artists at MadCatz could dream of. This newer joystick now feels like a $500.00 rig in comparison. And that prompted me to realize just how badly I had been conned out of $60.00 when this new Joystick was only $40 with a quality that just has no comparison.

If there is a joystick on the market that I would dub "Worlds Worst Joystick", Mad Catz, you get a 9999999 out of 10. You can't possibly get any worse without breaking the damn law. It is definitively impossible.

This company did indeed do one thing positive. I'll never stress again over a Mad Catz product because I will refuse to allow one in my house.

TL:DR: This is the most despicable, pathetic excuse for a $60 joystick I have ever had the misfortune to lay eyes upon. I hope someone finds trade or commerce laws that will burn the hell out of these guys because I'll be damned if they don't deserve every burn they'll get from resulting law suits, I mean that too, this joystick isn't a one-of-a kind defective batch, read the reviews. I kicked myself in the rear hard for not doing exactly what I just asked you to do.

And you know what, for the life of me, I can't understand how anyone having this joystick over a week can rate it more than 3 stars because I just don't buy that they were so incredibly lucky they happened to find some batch that was intended for the developers or investors of the company. I say this because there is no way the buttons didn't pop off or show a LOT of play in a demonstration without additional fortification to that little 1mm strip of flexiplastic that holds the button and provides all the pivot and bares 1/2 of the press pressure. Seriously, that's how bad it is.. 1mm. From what I can tell of the positive reviews, these people rated this joystick right out of the box without plugging it in or have only ever had this joystick and doesn't realize how incredibly low quality it is. When you get 40% slop while the stick is centered, most companies consider this defective, but read the instruction manual, not Mad Catz! They consider 80% slop defective!

REFRESHED REVIEW: Had to replace our previous GPS due to it being destroyed in a hit-&-run accident. So we get brand-spankin' new one out of the box. Power it up, annnnnnnnnd... the screen freezes on the first screen. So we update it through Garmin's update software. We update it three times (there seems to be a never-ending "Updates are Available" notice when we connect it to the PC). Power it up each time, annnnnnnnnn... the screen randomly freezes at various points from a few seconds to a couple of minutes after powering-up. It never stays unfrozen long enough to actually enter an address. So it's going back.

You know, when you use the manufacturer's software to do updates the manufacturer and the device tell you to do, you expect that activity not to render your GPS into a brick. But when that "bricking" event happens, the very least the manufacturer should do, is not simply tell you "out of warranty, so sad, too bad -- send us a c-note plus and we'll fix it". We didn't misuse it, we didn't drop it, there's not a scratch on it. GARMIN's software update killed it.

It starts, starts to load maps, and either shuts down immediately or tries to restart -- endlessly. Garmin has specific instructions on their website FOR THIS EXACT PROBLEM (so they KNOW about it already), but if their "fix" doesn't work, they simply tell you to fork over more bucks for you to send it in. CRAPPY, CRAPPY, CRAPPY CUSTOMER SERVICE FOR A CLEARLY DEFECTIVE DESIGN/UPDATE SYSTEM.

...update...

So my wife loves the GPS so much, that we simply bit the bullet and bought her a new one, and I kept the $130 refurbe'd unit for myself. Only... The 1st refurb unit came back with an intermittent pure-white screen (well, actually, the proper display was intermittent, it mostly displayed a pure-white screen). Got a 2nd refurb unit with their CRAPPY McCRAPPIN' 90-day refurb warranty window, and it's held up pretty well.

Well, my wife told me last week that hers has never had a solid traffic update connection. So we swapped cables and replicated the problem on my 2nd refurb unit. So I write the CRAPPY CRAPPER support at Garmin thinking "hey, I've got over two weeks before the 1-yr warranty mark, and you need to replace the crappy crap mccrappin' cable". But instead, the CRAPPY CRAP McCRAPPERS at Garmin tell me "Yeah no. That's outside your warranty" -- even though it's not. I mean I have the invoice, I can read a calendar, so unless God rewrote the calendar playbook, I *think* that 22 Aug 2016 is not the 1 year mark after 9 Sep which is the day we actually *received* the GPS to use it.

NEW & REFRESHED REVIEW (22 Jan 2019): Had to replace my previous GPS due to it being destroyed in a hit-&-run accident. So we get brand-spankin' new one out of the box. Power it up, annnnnnnnnd... the screen freezes on the first screen. So we update it through Garmin's update software. We update it three times (there seems to be a never-ending "Updates are Available" notice when we connect it to the PC). Power it up each time, annnnnnnnnn... the screen randomly freezes at various points from a few seconds to a couple of minutes after powering-up. It never stays unfrozen long enough to actually enter an address. So it's going back.

Now you might be asking: "Whyyyyyyyy do you keep buying one?" Two reasons:
1) Once it works, their user interface is better than any GPS that I've seen. Clean, simple, bright. (every OEM-installed GPS I've seen pales in comparison).
2) They brilliantly changed the cabling and accessory connections in their new GPSs. After buying/getting five GPSs from them, and having only two actually work out-of-the-box, we have enough auto accessories to keep in all three cars plus a travel set for rentals.

In case you're keeping track, they have a 40% reliability rate out of the box. I can't think of any product I've ever bought, that you have to plan on going through a purchase-return cycle at least once every time you buy.
top_profane.title: Great GPS -- IF you can get a working one (and be prepared for a warranty battle)

top_profane.text:
Bought this portable transistor radio and have used it now for four months, a long enough time to give it a fair test. Wouldn't buy it again. As with everything in life, there are good aspects to this radio and bad ones. For me, the bad ones have proven to outweigh the good ones.

First, here's what I think are its strengths. It offers a fairly loud audio volume if you need that, helpful if you use it in an environment that's noisy, perhaps at the beach or in the garage or in a work environment, for example. Also, I use the radio an average of an hour or more a day and I'm still on the original batteries after four months of use. I think that's remarkable. Then, it nicely offers an audio jack, something my stinking iPhone doesn't, so that could be convenient for some folks. Also, I've accidentally dropped the damn thing on the hard wood floor three times from a height of four feet, and the radio has not suffered any damage or been harmed in any way. It's quite robust, rather remarkable, too, in my opinion. Last, nothing has fallen off, loosened up or in any other way degraded over four months of use, so I suppose I ought to acknowledge that as well.

OK, now for the weaknesses. First, reception is, at times, just terrible. I listen to a local AM radio station whose broadcast antenna is just a few miles from my home. The station is a clear-channel, meaning it is one of the very few 50,000 watt stations in America. Did you get that? Fifty thousand watts. I can hear this station on my car radio 200 miles away when I travel. Yet, reception on the Sony ICF-F-10 radio at my home just a few miles from the transmitter is often muddy and plagued with interference, hissing and other general background crap. Not always, but way too often. Good reception is the whole point of a radio-- so you can clearly listen to what you're interested in, and this radio often performs its primary purpose very poorly. Next, the volume control on the radio is substandard. One can dial the volume down well enough, but only to a specific point. Then, the volume simply disappears. For me, I can't dial the volume down to a nice low level. It's either quite loud, or nearly off. There seems to be no capability for a low but still useable and desirable nice inside volume level. This has made the radio often unusable and truly infuriating to me. Next, the tuner inside the radio (the typical variable capacitor style) is crappy and insensitive. It is way too hard to tune in the signal, and the radio requires way too many minute adjustments of the tuning dial to get the station honed in clearly. Then, the signal constantly drifts so, once you've made all the extra efforts to dial in the station, the signal starts degrading and you have to constantly go back to the radio and re-tune the station. The constant retuning is just a whopping pain in the rear end. Also, the on-off switch is too small, a tiny little speck of a slide switch mounted on the side of the housing rather than a nice big rocker switch. It is simply too hard to operate. Plus, it also doubles as the AM/FM selector, which means that I sometimes unknowingly switch over to the FM band and get static when I think I just successfully turned on the radio and should be getting my regular AM station. Also, it is impossible to turn on the radio with one hand. The substandard switch design requires you to hold the radio in place with one hand and use the other to slide the difficult switch into the on position. One-handed operation is not possible, which is how I want to use the thing every time I turn it on. Then, there's the problem of where the switch is located-- right next to the audio jack. When trying to turn on the radio without looking at it, the feel of the audio jack and the crappy little on/off slide switch are identical, Over the last four months, I have more than once spent time trying unsuccessfully to turn on the radio until I stopped what I was doing and looked at things, only to discover I was pushing on the audio jack, not the switch. Just a crappy design element, an inexcusable poorly designed aspect of the radio. I suspect the radio's design dates back to the 1970s, something Sony long ago recovered its original design and engineering costs from.

Is the Sony ICF-F-10 radio useless? No, not at all. Sometimes it works just fine-- but only sometimes. Given all the other radios out there to choose from, I would think there are many more modern designs that would perform much better, especially given the fifty dollar cost this radio commands. If this radio were the only one on the market, it would be an acceptable buy. But, given that there is a mountain of other radios to choose from, Sony should have upped its game.
UPDATE:
I am adding to my review above now that I have been using the Sony ICF-F-10 portable AM/FM radio for a full year now. Remarkably, I am still using the original battery that I first installed a year ago. This is really quite surprising and amazing to me. For the great amount of audio volume that this radio can produce when you want, to have such amazingly low power consumption is shocking to me, and very nice. As for the reception, my biggest complaint about this radio, it remains deplorable. I find that it seems like the radio tunes in two stations at once. The first is the station I want to hear, which comes in loud and clear, the voices of the announcers and speakers being quite clear and pleasant. Superimposed over the desired radio station I want to hear is a second source, comprised of the most annoying static you could ever be forced to listen to. The static varies, changing in its tenor, quality and intensity in a random pattern that just makes trying to listen to the station you've tuned in impossibly irritating and troublesome. It just drives me crazy at times. Static and white noise are known among human-factors engineers to be among the most annoying and problematic sounds possible to the human ear, and the Sony ICF-F-10 portable AM/FM radio is just atrocious in this regard. My opinion of this radio is even worse now than when I first wrote my review above. Get a radio with a digital tuner and a modern radio antenna. Cell phones work in the radio frequency (RF) range and one rarely if ever has reception problems with the absolutely tiny RF antennas packed inside the very small case of a smart phone. This shows that GREAT RF radio reception is not a function of antenna size, and the Sony ICF-F-10 portable radio has a monstrous amount of space inside its case to fit a superb radio antenna with superb radio reception. The fact that this radio has such unacceptably substandard and horrible reception is a reflection of the terribly poor electronic design and the terribly cheap and poor components used in the manufacture of this terrible radio. Run, do not walk, away from this radio. What a piece of crap!

UPDATE NO. TWO:

OK, Here's the long term (two-plus years) update on this portable AM/FM radio. It is crap. There, I said it. That's all you need to know. It is total crap. It cannot tune in a clear signal to save its life. It is terrible. I just ordered new radio to replace it, some kind of non-branded Chinese radio that offers a digital display and digital tuning, (not a variable capacitor). I just got it today and I will offer a review when I've operated it for a while. The Sony I bought and painfully endured for far too long is going in the trash heap. A waste of money. But, it sure is efficient. Barely uses up the batteries at all.
top_profane.title: Works, but Not Very Well.

top_profane.text:
Wish I had read about this piece of crap before I spent $30 on this crap.
This is nothing but a glorified surge protector.

Moreover, it is useless.

Now let me explain. I bought this under the assumption that, per the title, that it intelligently autoswitches off devices when not in use. It does not do that!!

Rather, i was shocked to discover that instead of auto switching by detecting if the device was off, well instead all it does is switch off your other devices only when one of your devices is off (when it is plugged into a stupid "control" outlet). What a piece of garbage!!

So for example, if I turn off my computer (the "control") then I can't watch tv? What bull s#&*t!!! Ok so you say put that into one of the "CONSTANT" outlets (the red one)? Well then why the hell should you pay $30 for this if I plug the devices into outlets that NEVER SWITCH OFF???

It does NOT have any "SMARTNESS". In other terms, this is not digital - it is ANALOG. In other words, it only MANUALLY switches off devices. AND ONLY WHEN YOU HAVE ONE DEVICE TURNED OFF.

A much better way to make this MANUAL OFF power strip (it DOES NOT "autoswitch", this is MANUAL; the title is A LIE), the better way would be to put a switch on each outlet to manually toggle on or off to cut the power to each device individually. That would be more useful than this crap!

So in other words, what it does is nothing at all unless you turn off of main device, and is dependent on you not needing any other devices if that one device is off.

And you have three devices to plug in WHICH NEVER TURN OFF, which means YOU MIGHT AS WELL PLUG THEM INTO A REGULAR POWER STRIP, since those three red outlets DO NOT SWITCH OFF EVER.

I have these devices, and the number next to them is what each device uses, checked with Kill-A-Watt:
LED TV - 30w
PS3 - 110w
Computer - 100w to 200w dependent on usage
LED Monitor - 10w
Mini Fridge - 105w
Printer - 0w (it's off 99% of the time)

The ONLY way to use this "smart strip" crap is to use my PC as the "control". Because if I use anything else then it's going to shut off my PC which I can't have happen.

So in other words, THE SMART STRIP DOES ABSOLUTELY NOTHING FOR ME.

MOREOVER, I plugged everything (except mini-fridge) into a normal power strip (not even surge, just a cheap $5 power strip from Target that I bought 10 years ago), and plugged that into Kill-A-Watt. GUESS WHAT?? NO mythical "vampire drain" (ok it's not entirely a myth, but devices are made better nowadays and rarely drain uneccessary power. Vampire drain was a concept which mattered a lot last decade, but by this decade no longer is a relevant factor.). In fact, when plugged into my $5 power strip, my devices use ZERO watts used when devices are off!

Summary:
With everything plugged into a basic power strip and everything on, I use around 330w to 400w.
With everything off, I use around ZEROw. (NOT using this smart strip crap! This is with an old $5 power strip).

In other words, you're paying $25 extra FOR NOTHING! Return this and just use your normal power strip (or surge protector). You're NOT saving any money with this piece of junk!!

===========================

All that said, there are benefits to this device:
* It works as a surge protector (but not worth the price if all you want is a surge protector).
* EMI/RFI filtering to 52dB (although you get this in a good surge protector that is less expensive than this, just the same).
* The manual off of devices plugged into the green plug when you turn off the device plugged into the blue plug, does indeed work. (At least at first. Other reviewers said it stopped working after a short time like weeks or a month.)
* It looks fancy.
* It feels fancy.
* MAY be useful IF it continues working forever only IF you have a device or two which are draining power unnecessarily when off. Check it with Kill-A-Watt to be sure. Probably will not apply to you.

However it still gets one star because I wouldn't have bought this crap if I had realized in advance that there is NOTHING AUTOMATIC ABOUT IT! Instead I would have just bought a good surge protector, and for only half the cost of this crap.
top_profane.title: Don't be deceived by the lie "autoswitching"! NOTHING AUTOMATIC ABOUT IT!! This is ANALOG! Don't waste your money!

top_profane.text:
Monitor looks solid when OSX is poked and prodded into displaying it right. The price is superb for what you're getting, and despite a lot of early headaches (strictly on the end of OSX) I would gladly buy another.

The rest of this review is going to be a long and technical one, so preemptive tl;dr: it'll work with older Macs, including the 2012 Mac Mini (6,2), with LOTS of work and recovery boots and software changes and trial and error and frustration, and any fixes may be killed by Apple with future updates so you're probably on borrowed time.

Got it? Here's the nitty gritty if you are at your wit's end trying to get it to work on an older Mac Mini:

I have a 2012 Mac Mini, version 6,2. I wanted a 1440p monitor, and I wanted minimum 60Hz refresh. This Spectre seemed like it could work, and the price point was nice, so I went with it. Apple claims that the hardware will support 1440p over Mini Displayport, so everything checked out on paper. I bought an AmazonBasics MDP to DP cable, and when everything was delivered I went to work. And that's where everything fell apart.

Right off the bat, upon plugging everything in and powering on, the display was brought up as 1080p by default on OSX (10.15.5, Catalina). That sucks, I thought. So I went into Sys Prefs, Displays, and did the trick where you hold down the Option key while clicking "Scaled" to see the list of available resolutions. And wouldn't you know it? None were winners. And they shouldn't be; scaled is the worst thing you could do. It looks like trash. Text looks awful. Everything looks awful.

So this forks the problem solving route into several different directions. Monitor hardware settings? Computer software settings? Cable issues? Hallucinogens?

I ran through the available computer software settings (i.e. OSX's Playskool interface) and netted zero fixes. I ran through the available monitor settings via the keys on the backside of the Spectre and netted zero fixes. Okay... cable time. I had no independent way of knowing if the MDP to DP cable was working properly, so I just went on a tirade ordering cable crap from Amazon. Anything that could possibly fix the issue, any possible workarounds, any combinations... anything. I ordered another MDP to DP cable, a MDP to HDMI 2.0 cable, an ACTIVE adapter for MDP to HDMI, and an HDMI 2.0 cable. Plugged 'em all in... same general issues. Brass tacks: save the money, they won't work. If you want 1440p, at 60Hz, from Mini Displayport, your journey starts here.

So now the mission was to lift the veil of Apple's "it just works" BS operating system and look for some creative fixes via software. First up was an application called Display Menu. Upon launching, it shows all sorts of hidden resolutions that the monitor and cable combination are capable of displaying. Well, look at that! 1440p over MDP to DP! But... it's 30Hz. And upon selecting it... it's scaled. And looks like blurry crap. Same with HDMI. Same with MPD to HDMI. Same with active MPD to HDMI. Scaled crap.

Next up: SwitchResX! Much more robust interface, much more features, much more cost (it's shareware with a ten day trial)... but not much more solutions. Same cable arrays, same scaled blurry crap.

End of the line for these quick fix solutions. Cable swapping wasn't going to fix it, nor would simple interface applications. That leaves one final solution available: Terminal time! Don't like it? Tough. Your hand is forced.

You see, we need to back up a little and examine the primary issue at hand: Most obviously, Apple sucks. They hate their consumers, and they hate consumer choice, so guess what happens when the much hated consumers decide to exercise their much hated consumer choice on peripherals and venture outside of designated Apple products? Well, Apple gets all spiteful and makes any non-Apple products a nightmare to use with purposeful malicious coding. That is the case with monitors. Apple wants you to spend two thousand dollars on THEIR monitors, not 1/10th that price on someone else's monitors. So instead of making it all nice and logical and plug and play, Apple codes it so OTHER monitors are basically treated as lower quality TVs instead of high end monitor devices and therefore they are not afforded proper settings in EDID (Extended Display Identification Data, it sets how the display interacts with your computer). Then it piles on scaling and zero robust display controls in the system preferences, which makes it a horrible mess. If you were on Windows with basically identical hardware specs? You're gold. Just saying.

So with the problem isolated (probably), the goals became easier to define: attain RGB image, turn off scaling, get a native resolution from the monitor that looks good. Easy, right? Well, you're not out of the woods yet, because here's where conditions will separate the hopeful from the willful. The overarching parameters to success require some odd solutions.

First things first, you need to look up two things on google (that I will not link directly, because Amazon might delete this review if I do): "Force RGB Mode in Mac OS X to fix the picture quality of an external monitor", and "A script to fix EDID problems on external monitors in Mac OS". Pour through those. Read everything. Download the Ruby script on the latter's page. Secondly, you need to have SwitchResX downloaded. UNPLUG ALL OTHER MONITORS ASIDE FROM THE SPECTRE.

Got it? Okay, here's the major steps I undertook. (Please note that this is strictly what worked in MY particular case, and this information is purely educational with no suggestion whatsoever for you to do any of this; in other words, copying these techniques and bricking your machine is strictly a "You" thing and not a "Me" thing, so don't complain to "Me" if something goes haywire; this is a warning that "You" should NOT do the following, under any circumstances, unless you both waive me of any responsibility and can steel yourself to the possibility that you may end up with a paperweight of a computer if something goes wrong):
1. Follow the instructions to disable SIP
2. Follow the instructions to run the Ruby script. In order to actually move the resulting file into your Overrides folder, make sure to type:
sudo mount -uw /
3. Restart as needed
4. It'll STILL look like junk. The hell?!
5. Open SwitchResX, select the Spectre Z27 monitor on the left side tabs
6. Checkmark the line that says "Startup Resolution:" within the "Display Information" tab
7. Type "2560" into the first box right below, "1440" into the second, and "60" into the third (ignoring the quote marks, of course)
8. In the "Custom Resolutions" tab, click the + button towards the bottom and fill the resulting window accordingly (I included an image of what I put down, and it worked for me)
9. Quit SwitchResX, which will prompt you to save; make sure to save
10. Reboot
11a. Did it auto boot you into a nice looking 1440p 60Hz screen? Awesome! Done
11b. If not, open SwitchResX, go to the Spectre Z27 tab, go to the "Current Resolutions" tab, find "2560x1440 60Hz" in the list (it should be bold, and not have a checkmark under the "Scaled" column), click it, and pray for the best. Did it go blank and then refresh into a nice looking 1440p 60Hz screen? Awesome! Done
11c. If not, you've got big time issues and you're on your own (see why that dire warning before these steps is so important?)

That's pretty much it. If you read this whole thing, then it probably means that you got just as frustrated as I did trying to make this whole thing work. Hopefully it pans out for you. Again, this is not really a Spectre problem; it's an Apple problem. They're not a computer company anymore, they make phones and have basically ditched all aspects of care that goes into the desktop environment. It's really and truly time to move on to greener pastures, folks, and thankfully the "other side" of the computer market affords a much simpler plug and play landscape compared to this labyrinthian crap we have to deal with from Cupertino. This is coming from a guy that has been around since System 7.

UPDATE: I want to add some more final thoughts to this already exhausting review. A few facts need to be addressed to clean up some elements that were not covered well by me.

1. OSX Catalina basically completely obliterated LCD smoothing. There isn't a combination of workarounds out there that will fix the jaggedness and haloing of what you are seeing. This monitor will never look as crisp as, say, even a late 00's iMac screen. But you can get it *as good as possible* for a non-retina display with my previously mentioned steps if you are dead set on staying up to date on the operating system end.

2. The best possible image quality that you will get will be with older operating systems. Particularly with High Sierra. That's basically the last hoorah before Apple decided to crap all over everyone that didn't have an Apple retina display. Mojave will leave less than desirable results, but you can fix some problems. Catalina will look like trash, but will function. If you can't live with that, don't buy this monitor. Or do, and just buy a $200 Dell computer and get better results than what Apple's trash OS will limit you to.

3. The 2160p @30Hz that the monitor natively hits comes back to bite you, hard, when looking at potential middle ground solutions for display res. You could have gotten a crisp HiDPI rendering of 1080p through scaling, and it would have looked great... if not for the sluggish mouse and image movement rendering when limited to 30Hz. Unfortunate sort of bottleneck that destroys the "Plan B" that you may come up with.
top_profane.title: Complicated with older Apple computers (Mac Mini 2012)

Bag O' Crap. That big void where we toss caps, batteries, filters, cables, etc etc in the middle of shooting. We all have this space where we throw our random crap because the reality of photography is controlled chaos. I've always admired peak design products because they legitimately improve my workflow and help me manage the chaos of shooting. Need a carrying system without tangling yourself up in straps? Capture clip to the rescue! Need a strap that is easy to adjust and remove depending on the situation? Slide does the trick! Changing lenses constantly in the field becoming a risky hassle? Lens change kit is a god send! Need to change the set-up of your gear to accomodate different shooting situations? anchor links!....every....where.

I also own peak designs everyday messenger and and recently added the everyday backpack and while these bags are very thoughtfully designed, they don't seem to give much consideration to the chaos of real world shooting. It's as if the designers of these bags live in this utopian photography world where one can slow down neatly take place their random crap into these very specifically shaped pockets. I LOL at the idea! Perhaps we start by packing our bags this way, but when it comes to actually shooting, I find the whole concept sort of ridiculous. What I really need more than special pockets is a space to quickly throw my crap!

And so here enters the peak design field pouch....or as I call it, the field bag o'crap. What this thing is to me is a very modular bag or crap that can accomodate the crap accumulated in different shooting situations. In the middle of a shoot this thing is attached to my belt as a utility pouch (because that sounds cooler than fanny pack). When shooting casually and packing light with my Sony Rx1RII and nissin i40, I attach it to my slide lite and wear it like a mini everyday carry bag (because that sounds cooler than man-purse). When traveling I keep it in my everyday backpack or attach it to the outside of the bag using anchor links so I have a separate crap holder (because that sounds cooler than travel tote).

Long story short, the field pouch is simply a great bag o'crap. We all need one. Peak design made a good one.
top_profane.title: Great Bag O' Crap

This has been (yet again) one of the best buys I've EVER MADE of recent!!!!!!
This little thing almost rates right up there with your basic laptop of today!!
While my (spec-built) 5-year old laptop can never compare to today's computing standards, I've been beyond completely amazed when this little netbook outdid/outshone what I still considered to be top-notch in my 5yr old 17" laptop.
Especially when you consider it's running off a single processor (vs a dual cpu)!

Times have changed folks and if you're looking for something fun to trip around with ------- then THIS BE YOUR NETBOOK!!!!!!

Steer clear of the ATOM processor, the INTEL graphics, and even the Nvidia graphics....
... any netbooker should ONLY look at AMD and ATI as 'their choice'.
And that's not just me talking it up... that's each and every review I read saying the same thing.

Yes, of course the 'single processor' scared me, but upon arrival and everything since then ---- I'm thrilled. Sure, perhaps soon, some company will offer 'a build' for your perfect netbook... but until then expect nothing more than upgrading the memory (cpu's on acers on soldered onto the mobo). After all, this is what keeps the prices much lower than your 13" or even low-end 15" laptops.
.... thankfully, RAM is upgradeable to 4MB (should you need it) and the inclusion of a full OS (Win7 Home Premium) makes all the difference in the world when it comes to this item. Not to mention the full-size keyboard and the 'self-contained memory-dedicated graphics card'.

MY ONLY GRIPE:
I use this for many different reviews of speech-type scenarios.
And while I still plan to contact tech support about this issue, my fear is that there is NO FIX......
.... an MP3 plays 'sort of okay' (in regards to sound level)
.... windows sounds SUCKS (consider yourself lucky to hear a 'beep')
.... and most anything else falls into the 'sucks' category.
.... most sounds fall into the 'NIL' or 'CAN'T HEAR IT' category.

HOWEVER, A HOPE and A PRAYER does exist.... plugging into the 'headphones/speakers' jack -- the sound quality and volume jumps a good 80%+. I've had others confirm this due to my concern of 'me wanting it to be better'... but nope-yep... all seems to come out better via use of headphones and speakers. A further concern too was diminished by a prof recording engineer telling me that even if I choose speakers that only use the USB port, I should still get the same 'output quality' that I got by plugging into the USB + audio-outport otherwise. I had concerns that perhaps by using the USB port alone, that the sound quality AND volume would not improve no matter what I had bought (let alone the $$ spent) on USB-only speaker(s) vs something that plugged into both ports. I've been reassured all should be fine USB only; should this not be the case... I'll definitely post an update.

A week after receipt, I am attempting to figure out just which speakers to buy (and DO HOPE that they WILL increase & intensify the sound in a good way) but thought it worthy to share the 'WORST OF THE WORST' aspect of owning one of these puppies.
Should SOUND not be an issue (but honestly, when is it not?... heck, you own an 'electronic thing' -- one should be able to expect SOMEWHAT DECENT QUALITY OUT OF IT)!!!!

As one reviewer said "crap in, crap out" (a speaker review, not an ACER review).... I'm hoping this is NOT the case with an addition of good quality USB speakers (or even USB/audio out) speakers. Again... this will have to be a 'wait and see' issue. Even if I have to buy all 14 of my poss speaker choices here on amazon and then return 12-13 of them..... I might just have to do that in order to confirm my 'keeping of this purchase'. Personally, I'm thinking anyone else out there would have the same concerns as me (perhaps not as intense of a need, but still the same desires of 'decent' output sound-wise).

I do 'evidence analysis of very high and very low frequencies'... some come in as a very soft or low volume... others, a tad bit higher to start with. Due to this need which is the most important factor of 'JUST WHAT i TRULY THINK OF THIS NETBOOK' -- a FINAL REVIEW shall have to wait. I shall need a superiorly clear, increased volume, and -- most important, a PORTABLE sound with quality and clarity for my reviewing. If this IS NOT POSSIBLE, expect an update from me. I feel all should know of this netbook's 'lackings' before purchasing.... unlike me who just took a chance since I couldn't find the 'proof' otherwise :)

OTHERWISE, with the exception of sound, this puppy ROCKS!!!
BUT C'MON ACER... what the !@#$ are you doing jipping us out of anything close to decent sound quality found in a laptop/netbook's basic speakers!!!!
I just spent almost $500 bucks on a friggin netbook and it's beyond impossible to get any decent sound from it!!!?!?!?!????
ACER, I can't even hear my windows beeps or blurps or burps .....
Shame on you ACER!
I've still time to return this to you ACER.... here's hoping you're hearing your customer's gripes....

And yes, while a fab netbook ACER.....
and whether or not I keep it --- I shall not return to you for this any other kind of purchase until I've heard different of your audio practices :)
WE USERS EXPECT A QUALITY SOUND from a $430 system!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

DID YOU HEAR ME ACER!!!!
Yep.... $430 ain't chump change!!!!!
How's 'bout including a sound system that ACTUALLY PLAYS system sounds//windows sounds//my misc file sounds WITHOUT having to glue my ear to somewhere/anywhere on the laptop itself...... after all, you don't want some lawsuit from hundreds of us poor souls who had to glue their left or right ears literally to the the laptop in order to hear our next commands from windows or acer or, or, or, or?!?!??? This whole cancer and cellphone thing is sorta the same type of thing don'tcha think???
Weird eh?
Perhaps just an teeny-tiny upgrade to your internal speakers so that we're not all glued with our right ear 4 inches from the 'carcinogen-causing-megolithic-metallic-beast-we-call-acer-netbook-speakers' which could remain 'tinny-as-you-like-them' but at least powerful enough to hear a windows sound from across a dinner table without any outside (aka: plugged-in) speaker assistance. Dunno... just a thought acer.

And a further yep ----> while I believe this to be a 5-star product ACER... you've been downgraded to 4 stars (almost 3) in order to compensate for the assinine and incompetent audio system going on it such a high-end netbook.

top_rant.text:
First my background. I am an IT consultant responsible for deploying servers, workstations, and mobile devices. I have/had personally owned a tablet device that has 1 of the 3 major Mobile OSs. I had a ASUS Transformer for 30 days and liked it, I add the dock and like it...but didnt love it. I have a number of iOS device Touch, Pad, Phone. And now the Webos powered TouchPad (TP). I like them all in different ways but the touchpad seems to be what I use most. maybe because it is the newest...but there is something about it that I like. It seems as if Apple and Google had a child this would be it. It has many of their strengths but not many of there weaknesses...it has its own weaknesses but much because it is new. So lets start and look at these devices to see what makes them different and which one stands on in its respective category. This is also a post that is going on my blog for it has taken me a long time to test and review these product before I could write this.

First...keyboard...a way to interface with a device is critical. Touchscreens have all became pretty similar in regards to pinch to zoom, scrolling, selecting, etc. But the keyboard is something that has been why I think so many are scared to get into tablets. Sure they lack that tactile feel but some do a better job then others. So with that said the onscreen keyboard on the touchpad is my favoirte of all 3. For many reason, first is onscreen keyboard more so the users make far more mistakes. So have a spell checker is a must...android/honeycomb...they skipped out on this...big mistake. Not to mention visually the keyboard is unappealing. It makes tons of mistakes and seem the least forgiving. Apple...very good the original, inline spell checker, responsive, polished but after using the TP it is in second place. The Touchpad keyboard has the number keys above...something the iOS does not. The TP keyboard is comfortable the sounds it make are not intrusive and just plan works...the 3D key look is nice not to mention the transitional. So in the interface department the TP seems to be well thought out and engineered.

Multi-tasking on the webOS tablet...touchpad...is light years ahead, iOS is plain horrible and is not a true multitasking system it uses a handful of "services" mostly notification to make it feel as if it isnt multi-tasking. Double pumping the middle button make the multi-tasking menu of running apps appear. To close them is terrible and not well thought out. Apple needs to go back to the drawing board!!! Android/Honeycomb is good and much better than iOS but it is still unorganized and completely unpolished! Android gets sooo choppy when memory usage approaches its limits. Honeycomb is much better then froyo but still not that great in comparison to the webos TP. The card system is just better...its intuitive, simple, functional, smarter just plain easier to switch between and flat out know what is running! It is as I have already said light years ahead!

Account integration is far better on the TP. The others seem to require more apps to get more accounts up on the device. Out of the box the TP has tons of account integration. I dont need "An app for that" I use one app for email and grab 6 of my accounts...android/honeycomb...ah no. I check, Hotmail, two Gmail accounts, and a Yahoo account on it. I use one email app on the TP...its native. And set it up on in a combined inbox. iPad...yes it work very similar like the TP but the function is a little nicer in my opinion on the TP. Android is just a mess..I need the gmail app for my gmail, the yahoo app for my yahoo (Froyo...honeycomb now does a little better job on this) and the built in email app for hotmail. Sloppy! But the email app on honey is quite nice looking and replying back I like a little better on it...but once again the lack of spell checking is just stupid on behalf of google! But before I stray off. Account integration also steams throughout the entire webos...they call it synergy. What ever they call it I like it! When I setup my 2 gmail account it automatically added them to my Message app on the TP. Not to mention I added a skype account to the messaging app as well. Oh and did I mention calendar...it does those as well. It also integrate throughout webos, Facebook, LinkedIn, Photobucket, Box.net, Dropbox, yahoo, aol, the list goes on and on! Android and apple dont even come close! But that still isnt to say the TP is missing. Dropbox is limited and not every aspect of these account is fully put into the TP...but it is still more then the others!

IMing...TP kills all!! I need multiply 3rd party apps on iOS and Android. And 3rd party free apps have ads!! Oh and most 3rd party free apps I hate to say it lack polish! My one messaging app on TP does, skype, yahoo, google, and AIM!!! And it does them well!! The interface works isnt garbage up and runs as it should. eBuddy...not even close! I have tried every IMing app for both iOS and Android and they just arent close!

Web-browsing. TP once again kills it. My transformer even after the update force closes!! I do like the tabs in Android...really nice but they get choppy and hard to navigate if you have too many open...becomes a pig! And Apple...dont get me started...slow and plugin missing black screens...Apple really dropped the ball here. But then again you can ususally get an app for many of the sites that dont work...but at what cost and for the love of god how many apps do I really want and need!!! it gets a little stupid after a while. The apps are nice for they are engineered and optimized for a tablet experience. Some more of many lack. But they help bring the web experience missing back to the iPad...but it doesnt bring everything back! Many of the major sites are re-written to work with the iPad. My company and many other cant afford to re-write our sites to HTML 5 to support the iPad...it is expensive and apple should have done a better job. Sure there is Skyfire but it is nowhere near that good! The Touchpad is as close to a desktop browser as a tablet can come! Flash isnt everything but it still makes up over 50% of the website on the internet!! My company site included! Bookmarks are nice on the TP but I do wish it had tabs. Everytime you want a new site to open you need a new tab, unless you are just working from the original...not really a problem but I tend to have several tabs open on my desktop, usually 15-25 at a time. The tablet I dont typically do this.

Notification system. In this order TP, Android, Apple. Apple is just plan terrible...intrusive, annoying and just old...iOS5 is changing this...but that is yet to be released. As of now TP notification system is organized, intuitive, elegent, visual and most important doesnt get in the way. It works at the lock screen as well! Android is decent but still sloppy when compared to webos. I like how clean the TP system works. My iProducts drive me nuts with the current notification system...email...pops up right over what you are doing...click "view" or click "close"...view takes you away from what you are doing. "Close" gone into thin air! it plain sucks! Android as I said is better then apple but cluttered again and disorganized! I still would take it over apple but it needs some work.

Apps...TP is dead last...much because it is 6 weeks old compared to the others. But more of a concern is since HP is giving the device side a dirt nap what happens to the software side. webos was never given enough time to interest developers now this is even worse considering the current state of webos. A big black eye. However since the web browsing isnt so crippled I can do a ton without the need for many apps...just like I do on my Mac or My Win7 box...I dont need a ton of apps on it. 90% of what I do is via a browser! Apple is an absolute app dream...there are over 3000 weather apps...many garbage but definitely a few more then impressive ones! There apps are typically more polished feature rich and tablet or phone optimized. I love pulse!! There are sooo many but sometimes navigating and remember which ones I have is a pain! Honeycomb...well not much better then the TP. Although I do have pulse, new360, teamviewer. Apple is light years ahead...the quality is excellent and these apps do more and increase the capability of the iProducts so beyond that any have imagined! But then again I give the iPad a 4 1/2 overall as well...the app part is a 5 all day long!!! But remember this...apple apps usually cost money...newspaper, magazines...etc. Some stuff you can usually get online free. But as I said it is stll more then that.

Hardware...HP is dead last. But not horrible. Rear camera is missing...never used mine and my transfomer, not sure if because it sucks or I just dont carry my tablet around to take pictures. I do use the front for skype and googletalk. No expansion...once again I never found a need for it, not on apple of tp. But the android tabs have it. The android tablets I believe run away with this because there are so many different version of it. For example the ASUS has a dock...that also has a battery in it. Keyboard is nice and it even has a mouse. So it feel like e netbook. The docks keyboard is stellar! Not to mention optimized for honeycomb with hotkeys. Battery life was excellent on the Ipad and the Asus. As for TP, good but not as good. The more cycles the better it got. It is about an hour to 90 mins less then the other. The asus with the dock is ridiculous...tons of time. Processor on the TP is slower and feels a little slower but not horrible and doesnt seem to feel like it is getting slower like the asus did when too many apps where loaded. The Apple screen is amazing but then again so is the asus...in fact the asus screen I like more! TP last in screen, it isnt as bright but cant say I have ran into anytimes that I thought it wasnt bright enough. Speakers..the TP kicks the crap out of both...no match! It does lack expansion ports but so does apple and they seem to be fine without it. I am yet to use the ports on the asus. But I do like the touchstone dock...quick easy and works great! And I also like the fact that the charging cable is not exclusive to the device...couch couch apple and asus. I can use any microUSB cable to charge or plug in the TP. I have used my samsung charger to charge my TP...yes it works fine! I do not like the glossy finger print magnet shell on the TP. But I put the HP rubberized case with easel on it and never looked back. Yes it is heavy and thicker but it is nominal at best. These tablets are so light and I am not 90 years old. This not even worth a talking point.

Now a few areas of pluses and minus for the touchpad in comparison. For starters the backup function is awesome!!! Right to the cloud for your apps, settings, contact, cal, etc. No movies, music, or phones...which would have been cool but replacement or coming off a reset is very quick to restore. Android...pass...it is start over city, yeah there are a few apps to back it up the the SD card but it is technical and I have seen android eat SD Card...especially rooted devices! Apple...with itunes (Which is a love hate handcuffing situation) is nice from a restore standpoint. Easy to restore to a new device or restore from a reset device. And it resets everything! But you need to do it all via itunes. This is changing. The copy, paste, highlight absolutely sucks on the TP!!!! I hate it...the others are killing it here. I wish it had widgets..I am not missing them that much on the TP but some I did like on android. Apple...none. The calendar is sluggish on the TP and I really like the android and apple ones better. I only use one calendar so I dont see the benefits as well as other might with the TP. For the TP handles multi-cals pretty good. No push for Hotmail kinda sucks but I can wait 5 minutes. Just type is pretty nice. It scours the entire device and can also scour sites if you like. I cannot do this on the others. It is a nice way to weed thru the crap faster.

All in all for a now defunct product that can be had for 99 or 149 this is a fantastic tablet. I ripped some movies via handbrake, copied my music, check my email, surf, and IM this is a stellar device. If you need certain apps this is not for you. I dont miss them and I find myself using this more then the other, could be because it is new but I like the web on it better. I like email on it better. I actually like music and movies on it better as well. Oh and the facebook app is soo much nicer then the others, No ads and it is polished! For the core functions it has in common with the others. It is a better device in many ways. The interface doesnt feel fisher price like my ipad does and iphone. It is clean, organized, intuitive and smooth. Too bad it is leaving the party early for I think it had a future. HP just blew something in the hardware that might have made it a stand out.

SECOND UPDATE:
----------------------
I purchased this TV on April 20, 2020
Today is December 08, 2020
For the last month on and off, I have noticed that the TV will do this blinking thing where it will randomly shut the display off for a half a second and the display comes back. I can overlook it but I advise potential customers to heed this warning. This is not a bad TV but if this is what the display does after 8 months.... Idk what its gonna do at a year and a half. Will continue to update!!! At this point the TV went from a 5 star rating to a 2 star rating.

========================================================================================

FIRST UPDATE:
-------------------
Mounting this TV was a hassle but still with all the hassle I went through, I still stand by my 5 star review. I will tell you how to bypass the nonsense to make it as hassle free as possible.

IMPORTANT INFORMATION:
>> Vesa Mount Configuration: 100mm x 100mm
>> Screws needed for mounting: M6 x 12mm

MOUNTING INFORMATION FOR TCL 43S425 43 Inch (2018) [DIFFERENT SIZES OF TV MAY/WILL REQUIRE DIFFERENT SIZE SCREWS AND VESA MOUNTS]

THE PROBLEM:
* The mounting bracket that came with my original mount setup for my mount aligned perfectly, but the screw holes were too small (M4 x 12mm).....

* Only 2 screws came with the TV and I had to buy additional screws myself and figuring out what size the screws were was a hassle (There were many screws and all of the screws were not in their right places). I went to AutoZone and took the screws that came with my TV. The actual size of the screws that are inside the TV already and the screws you will need to buy are M6 x 12mm, you will need to buy 2 more screws. In total there should be 4 screws, 2 that came with your TV already and 2 additional screws you have to purchase. THERE IS A STRONG POSSIBILITY THAT MOUNTING THIS TV WITH ONLY 2 SCREWS WILL CAUSE YOUR TV FALL OR DAMAGE YOUR SCREWS. PLEASE MOUNT WITH 4 SCREWS!!!!

THE SOLUTION:
* I used a bigger drill bit to drill into the holes on my bracket so that the screws from my TV would fix into the bracket (takes about 10 minutes of work with my crappy barely powered drill but it got the job done, please make sure to drill the holes in a well ventilated area and wear a face mask because the metal becomes very dust-particle-like.)

* Go to a hardware store / your local auto parts store (I went to AutoZone) and purchase a "Need a Part" screw.... If you don't have time to look at all the numbers.... For AutoZone the item number is 441960A it comes in a lime green box with a blue triangle on the bottom. It's going to say M6-1.0x10mm. Class 8.8 HEX HEAD CAP SCREWS. (This is the only ones I was able to find at my local store, your store may have an even better selection.)

* You will need a socket wrench OR just some good old pliers and of course... Some elbow grease.

vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
ORIGINAL REVIEW:
^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
FYI: This review (and all of my reviews) are 100% real reviews and are not for purchase. (I do this to make sure that consumers like myself can get a REAL representation of the product since many sellers buy reviews now a days, someone has to keep things honest.)
=======================================================================================
PURCHASE INFORMATION:

TV PURCHASE DATE: April 20, 2020
RECEIVED TV: April 22, 2020
PRODUCT PURCHASED: TCL 43S425 43 Inch 4K Ultra HD Smart Roku LED TV (2018)
TV SIZE: 43 Inches
DELIVERED TO ME BY: UPS (Was delivered faster than it actually said, which is great!)
=======================================================================================
PRE-REVIEW NOTATION:
---------------------------------
(To help you with your decision, coming from a legit customer....) I am extremely glad I made this purchase, I feel like I made out like a bandit in this deal. My ex has a Samsung TV (65 inch though) and he paid so much more money than I did and this TV quality is right on par if not better than my ex's Samsung TV. Just because this brand might not be familiar to you, don't let it deter you from getting this TV. I am extremely happy with my purchase!!!!!!

I don't get paid to do reviews (I don't do YouTube reviews as of yet).... I wouldn't sit here and type all of this out if I didn't stand by my purchase decision!)

MORE FOOD FOR THOUGHT:
On October 26, 2018 - I purchased a (VIZIO - 32" Class - LED - D-Series - 1080p - Smart - HDTV - Model: D32F-F1
SKU: 6202310) from Best Buy for $219.99....... This TV was literally the worst TV I ever purchased and it's a VIZIO (A known name brand now a days.).....

I paid almost the same amount of money and got a trash TV..... I had problems with my Vizio since day 1!!!! Yes, there's a record of me already reaching out to Vizio on October 26, 2018. I even had a repair tech come out to "fix" my TV (TV wasn't fixed)..... I paid $219.99 and my TV is 32 inches and it's not even 4K and it's super slow and apps barely respond!!!!!

You'd be paying around $250 and you are getting 4K, it's 43 Inches (massive yet very slim), super responsive (super fast), and there are so many other things that this TLC has that my Vizio could NEVEEERRRR even think of having!

TLC is clearly aiming to take Vizio's customers.... My last run in with Vizio has been a terrible one and THAT'S why I bought this TLC TV and I'M EXTREMELY GLAD I DID!!!!!!

I like this TV so much, that I'll be buying another one to replace my other 32 Inch TV that I use as a monitor as long as the price stays the same!!!!!

I PROMISE YOU, YOU WILL NOT BE DISAPPOINTED!!!!
THIS TV IS A TOTAL PACKAGE (AND THEN SOME)!!!!

=======================================================================================

PROS:
* PRICE ($246 with tax in Illinois) - Very affordable.
* CLARITY - The display is very crisp
* RICH COLOR - Rich blacks, bright whites, great color!!!!
* FAST RESPONSE (Not refresh rate) - This TV responds IMMEDIATELY to the commands on my phone / physical remote. No stuttering in the menus, no lag.
* 4K - This 4K is good enough to play with my Xbox One S and have a good time doing it!
* COMPUTER MONITOR - This TV works good as a computer monitor (I use it for my Xbox, Nintendo Switch, and my Gaming PC) - I have NOT measured the refresh rate and don't know how to do that. But my game play does not suffer at all (I play my games / PC hardwired into my Comcast / Xfinity router).
* USER INTERFACE (UI) - Is very intuitive and time saving. You can even easily name input names (instead of having HDMI 1, HDMI 2, HDMI 3..... I have HDMI 1, Computer, and Xbox (You don't even have to type the names in, they have them pre-programmed which is super super nice!)
* 3 HDMI PORTS - Huge plus!
* ROKU REMOTE APP (On Google Play Store / Apple App Store) - Chances are.... your TV remote is not by you... but your phone is.
* ROKU CHANNELS - Watch free TV / Movies (Way better than Crackle and Hulu with commercials because the commercials barely exist!)
* AMAZING SCREEN SAVERS!!!!!! - *This next part has to be written in CAPS LOCK because of how good it is* THERE IS A HUGE LIBRARY OF PAID AND FREE SCREENSAVERS!!!!!! Even the free screensavers are amazing like the aquarium one!!!! You can have a fireplace on your TV!!!! That is amazing!!!!! TLC obviously put some TLC into what their customers expect from a TV and they have NAILED IT!!!!!
* SETTINGS - Even the settings menu is intuitive!
* TV STAND / FEET - The TV stands firmly and does not wobble unlike my previous Vizio. That is a very nice touch!!!
* BUILD QUALITY - The build quality is superb! Feels super sturdy yet slim!!!!
* SHIPPING TIME - My TV came faster than I was told!!!!!

CONS:
* Literally NOT A SINGLE CON (yet). I know that technology tends to fail over time and heavy use. I will update this review as soon as anything changes (but I honestly don't feel like it will any time soon.)

top_rant.text:
EDIT: GO TAKE A LOOK AT THE ACTUAL PICTURES NOT THE PHOTO-SHOPPED ONES ANTEC MADE OF THE PRODUCT I INCLUDED ON THE AMAZONE PAGE.

FIRST: Take a look at the picture on Amazon's site for this item, On Antec's site, and on Buy.com's site. Looks great right?? Nice VFD display...(just like on the Antec Elite model) NOPE!!! NOPE!!! NOPE!!! Sure, read the fine print for the Premier model (this model) it's an LCD display. BUT NOT JUST ANY LCD DISPLAY IT'S A TINY / CROPPED \ WASHED OUT LCD DISPLY. Why does this matter? Well I expected a display was relatively close to what Antec, Amazon, and Buy.com all advertise. That display Looks Awesome!! (BTW the Display that the ANTEC ELITE (the MODEL BELOW THIS HAS).

The actual display has a very narrow angle before it begins distorting (like some LCD monitors do. So unless you are looking dead on to 10-15 degrees to the left or right. This thing is going to look WASHED out & UGLY at 45 degrees i can see all the hidden buttons around the display and the display looks almost WHITE, the display of text at that point is nearly un-readable.

Just a FLUKE with my model (NOPE!!!) Yes you can make minimal contrast adjustments but it only allows you to go from unacceptable to down right terrible! even dead on looks washed out & NOTHING, REPEAT NOTHING LIKE THE PICTURES! =============

Next issue, this unit couples or attaches inline with the Power supplies 20/24 pin main power motherboard connection. What's the problem with that? Well unlike the cable from the power supply which has solid construction and the pins don't move around / secured pins, this coupler does not, it's my only complaint with the initial setup. So it can make the insertion like attempting to herd barn cats. Think about a LOOSE pinned molex connector but instead of 4 loose pins it's 6 x that, I required two small needle nose players to get the connections to mate properly. Without that it pushed the pins out the wrong way not making a proper connection & therefore couldn't snap lock the units together. (YMMV)...
The rest of the hardware installation was straight forward. OTHER than don't bother installing the drivers off the tiny CD. Go to Antec's site & download everything from there. It MAKE sure to go through the settings & select for it to automatically check for updates, if not selected by default. I had to restart before I could get it to go out & check and YES, there are additional updates even after installing the latest offering from Antec (As of 1-2010).

[[AT THIS POINT I WOULD HAVE BEEN FAR HAPPIER HAVING BOUGHT THE ANTEC ELITE MODEL]] The Display was supposed to be the pizazz the cache' of this item. The wow factor & i'm super BUMMED by how plain & ugly it is. See that EQ picture that they show... NOPE!! Sure there is an EQ, but it looks nothing like that!!!!

A positive: The Remote control's ability to turn on & off my PC awesome!

But lets get back to the LCD screen for a bit. It's got so many areas outside of the center of the screen that light up with various features that you end up subtracting 1" off the left & right side of the screen and 1/2" of both the top & bottom of the screen. And while you can make SOME Font & Size adjustments. There are Some LONG words that Can't be fully displayed all at once as it tracks across the screen even at tiny TEXT SIZE that's RIDICULOUS. Well what I can read by simply looking at the news displayed by the software You can read it right on the desktop when i bring that part of the included software up in say 4 seconds, but it takes 1 - 2 minutes to scroll across the tiny LCD screen. First I would like to ask that you **PLEASE** read MY WHOLE review/informative comments about the product and being an investigator.
1.)BE PATIENT! you are NOT going to get something RIGHT AWAY maybe not even your first 10 tries, especially if you don't do the groundwork first
2.)BE KIND!! (Look for ways to help others, spirits -- not just scare yourselves)
3.)SEEK HELP! FIND AN EXPERIENCED GROUP (if new talk to local Paranormal Research Groups in your Area >> Just GO ONLINE they shouldn't be hard to find. They WILL HELP YOU, THEY WILL MOST LIKELY LET YOU WORK WITH THEM ON INVESTIGATIONS THEY ARE DOING (you will have to be the low man/woman on the totem pole and just listen and adsorb everything around you. DON'T BE AFRAID TO ASK QUESTIONS
4.)AND FIND A MENTOR** (this is important, because as you progress - they will have progressed too - and it's good to have someone (preferably outside your group, but not necessarily) to bounce ideas off of, find new ways of attacking a problem, just have a friend (sometimes it's really tough, if you choose to do this "professionally"
5.)ALWAYS USE MORE THAN JUST ONE DEVICE. in the review I read a lot of people are running around because they got this, saw it on a show and gosh darn, it should be saying things that dead people say! But, of COURSE that's just not they case, you have to do your research. I found AN AMAZING ONE (HE KNOWS WHO HE IS <3) and LET THEM SHARE THEIR EXPERIENCE AND GUIDE YOU the way mine did for me and helped me found my own group!! 17 years now we've been at it!! Would not have been possible WITHOUT A MENTOR!
5.)ALWAYS USE MORE THAN JUST ONE DEVICE. in the review I read a lot of people are running around because they got this, saw it on a show and gosh darn, it should be saying things that dead people say! But, of COURSE that's just not they case, you have to do your research. I found AN AMAZING ONE (HE KNOWS WHO HE IS <3) and LET THEM SHARE THEIR EXPERIENCE AND GUIDE YOU the way mine did for me and helped me found my own group!! 17 years now we've been at it!! Would not have been possible WITHOUT A MENTOR!
6.)DO YOUR RESEARCH****** (Critical)****, NOT NECESSARY FOR THOSE OF YOU WHO ARE EXPERIENCED SKIP DOWN A PARAGRAPH
****STEPS*****
1.) Dig online, 2.)GO TO THE LOCAL LIBRARY (a lot of the older records are not yet online, especially pertaining a specific property - have the librarian help you so you can help yourself in the future at other libraries where your
ghost exploration" takes you, 3.) do interviews with local people - see what the urban legends are associated with the property, 4.)see if you can find a witness - either someone who lived there or was a VERY GOOD friend or family member of someone who was, have the current owner walk you through the house - see if they and others can give you a feel for the hotspots to look out for that night. 5.)(Have your gear set up no later than 4pm) 6.)get ALL your research together ESPECIALLY A FLOOR PLAN and 7.) FORM A GAME PLAN THAT AFTERNOON, 8.) MAKE SURE EVERYONE KNOWS THEIR TASKS SO YOU AREN'T BUMPING INTO EACH OTHER... If it is JUST YOU do ALL the same steps, 9.) try to talk someone into going with you so you have a witness (and hopefully partner down the road - if they REALLY DO NOT WANT TO GO DON'T PUSH as it leaves them open to spirits (more so), try not to get "drawn" to a particular place - (((if you investigate a VERY ACTIVE location over and over IT WILL START TO AFFECT YOU AND YOU CAN HAVE ATTACHMENTS AND NOT EVEN KNOW IT!)) 10.) Get your team assembled BEFORE entrance but AT THE LOCATION WITH A FLOOR PLAN (for each member) 11.) Go over GAME OF ATTACK ONE MORE TIME 12.) BE CAREFUL 13.) Review Evidence (carefully, ideally if time by 2 members for each piece of audio/visual etc) 14). Debrief yourselves, discuss any issues or questions within the group 15.) Debrief the client (truthfully)

**************************************************************************
There WAY MORE BEAUTIFUL some beautiful experiences I've had with this device, I actually have 3 for my investigative unit to use when we are separated at a location) and ALL have been successful. As just one ex. I was actually able to connect a local lady to her departed husband, he was holding on because they'd had harsh words before his heart attack (she had NO IDEA and didn't even remember his *harsh words, but he was hanging onto them and it was keeping him earth-bound). They forgave each other and he was able to get PEACE! It was **SO VERY BEAUTIFUL***. AND THEY WERE BOTH ABLE TO LET GO!! And we've done this type of thing AGAIN AND AGAIN! SOOOOO Much more satisfying than just seeing what goes BUMP in the next abandoned asylum (although TRUTH: It's FUN TOO :) But TRY to HELP the ghost there NOT JUST SCARE yourselves! USE this DEVICE and SEE WHAT THEY NEED!!!!)

I've ALSO FOUND THE NASTY SIDE OF IT NASTY! In once instance we were prepared and helped kids, in one were were NOT and it had ramifications...The FIRST is where we DID HELP. Local teenagers were messing with a spirit in a VERY old abandoned swimming pool & had grafftied pentagrams (they thought it was just funny, not realizing the power in such symbols. The parents called because the kids started changing...(thinking they were just playing with a ghost and it ended up being a demon we had to have exorcised, but in doing so, I BELIEVE MY TEAM SAVED SOME OF THOSE KIDS FROM OPPRESSION OR WORSE!! SOME WERE ALREADY SHOWING SYMPTOMS OF IT. That was 9 years ago and those "kids" now are starting families of their own. That makes me thankful!!!

Then of course there was the time we were a NEW team and thought we could DO ANYTHING. We had just pooled our money, bought our gear and talked ourselves into something we were WHOLLY UNPREPARED for because we'd NOT DONE OUR RESEARCH, INCITED THE GHOSTS and WE WERE TOO COCKY!

We went to an area (I'm not going to name it b/c people in my location will recognize my name) anyway, this was EARLY ON, we hadn't protected ourselves SPIRITUALLY, EMOTIONALLY or PHYSICALLY for what we might encounter. We thought.."Whooo spooky place, let's get them riled up and try to get evidence," RIGHT? Well, BAD idea... (You see them do it on shows like Ghost Adventures, but they've back off a little -- but even whn they do it, they've protected themselves both beforehand and afterwards - WHAT THEY DON'T SHOW (but maybe once or twice, in later shows) and they STILL have ATTACHMENT ISSUES!)

Well, we were NOT PREPARED AT ALL and yes, had the bejesus scared out of us, had several members scratched, pushed and hit and had one member who had an attachment that almost cost him his marriage. It took OVER A YEAR to get rid OF THE ATTACHMENT AND THIS WAS NO JOKING MATTER!!! So don't go barging around like Ghost Rambo! If you don't know what you're doing!

************************************************************************************
This Spirit Box (along with others) is ONE of the MOST import tools in my arsenal! And THAT's ANOTHER thing people need to understand. YOU HAVE TO HAVE MORE THAN JUST HIS CHASING GHOSTS!!! P-SB7 SPIRIT BOX IS A GREAT PRODUCT (A TOOL IN ANY PARANORMAL RESEARCHERS KIT (probably one of the most important, because once you've identified the hot spots, if your equipment or your body is telling you something is near, THEN you fire this bad boy up (telling this to less experience people and the ones leaving negative feedback because they think they are Zak Baggins ;)

****OF COURSE it's not going to be just like TV -
#1.) THEIR EQUIPMENT ISN'T GOING TO BE <$100 on AMAZON OK? They work with engineers and have THEIR VERY OWN VERSION of a SPIRIT BOX just because they call it their "Spirit Box SPX7" DOES NOT MEAN IT'S THE SAME THING OK? GOT IT YET?

#2.) THEY EDIT **HOURS AND HOURS of NOT GETTING ANYTHING (sometimes they only show what they got on their EVP after running a Spirit Box or other Communication Device Session) and ONLY SHOW WHAT THEY DID GET (or else it would be a VERY, VERY boring show, it's one of my FAVS too....)

***BUT MORE IMPORTANTLY YOU ALWAYS HAVE 2 THINGS WITH YOU WHEN INVESTIGATING. #1 Extra SCIENTIFIC INSTRUMENTS I mentioned (you can even get books on the topic, don't just go buying stuff) but #2 YOUR BODY**** (your body can pick up JUST as much as the instruments. If you start feeling something, have it confirmed with a device (depending on what you are feeling i.e. cold spot you should be monitoring the ambient temperature throughout the night, so if a cold spot DOES HAPPEN it is EASY to DOCUMENT SCIENTIFICALLY. TO HAVE MORE THAN ONE DEVICE ONE TO TELL YOU IF YOU'RE IN THE RIGHT AREA (I prefer the K2 (to tell me if I'm in the right area) AND WHEN YOU ARE RUNNING THIS OR TALKING TO ANY SPIRIT, YOU SHOULD HAVE AT **LEAST** 1 GREAT QUALITY DIGITAL RECORDERS

PLEASE DON'T TRY USING YOUR PHONE! (some unnamed ridiculous shows on Destination America --that Also have some of my Favorites/that and TRAVEL CHANNEL) SHOW them using their phones, and doing auto play back....what they DON'T TELL YOU IS THAT THEY ALL HAVE THE MOST EXPENSIVE PHONES and get $$ for naming them and they're **LOADED w/ SPECIAL APPS THAT HELP.

BUT A GOOD DIGITAL RECORDER BEATS A PHONE HANDS DOWN 100% of the TIME!!!- but you should have 2 things (again), a SCIENTIFIC INSTRUMENT and equally important YOURSELF (your body will tell you things if you listen to it. Once you've been on enough of these - at first your adrenaline is too high to REALLY let you LISTEN to your body, you've got to ****CALM YOURSELF THE FIRST FEW TIMES****.

***I AGAIN want to say: BE PATIENT, BE KIND, USE MULTIPLE TOOLS, DON'T GIVE UP AFTER THE FIRST TIME, RESEARCH AREAS WITH HIGH ACTIVITY AND SEEK OUT OTHERS IN YOUR AREA WITH THE SAME INTERESTS!!!!

***PROBLEMS w/ (most not all) OF THE LOWER #1 vs 5 STAR RATINGS if you take the time to read them (and I did)

1.) Our own ego (I had it at first too, so I'm not pointing fingers!!!) Were expecting to walk out like Zak Baggins on Ghost Adventures and just get a reply right away not understanding (although he's said it MANY times of the show) that they got through HOURS of NO RESPONSE AT ALL WITH ANY OF THEIR VARIOUS Ghost Communication equipment
2.) BIGGEST PROBLEM BY FAR, not patient enough to realize that the WHOLE WORLD isn't HAUNTED ALL OF THE TIME! It takes patience and research!! You can just go somewhere (like a graveyard on one night for an hour and try it and declare THIS IS A PIECE OF TRASH, IT DOESN'T WORK! Ghost Hunting DOESN'T WORK THAT WAY. Seek people in your area with REAL EXPERIENCE and ask for help or to come on some of their "hunts" - in my experience the field IS ALL ABOUT SHARING (Yes, it gets competitive too, but, even those who are competitive SHARE and help each other, new comers etc.
3.) I'd argue that THIS IS ALSO #1 MOST IMPORTANT **PATIENCE, PATIENCE, PATIENCE!** Ghost "Hunting" or I prefer Exploring, takes A LOT of time and A LOT of review TIME!
****YOU ALSO HAVE TO HAVE YOUR DIGITAL RECORDERS RUNNING BECAUSE IT MAY NOT BE ON THE WAVELENGTH OF THE SPIRITBOX RIGHT THEN BUT YOU VERY WELL MIGHT CATCH AN EVP (this has happened to me A LOT!) I have A LOT of stuff on YOUTUBE, BUT AM NOT POSTING IN CASE I'VE ANGERED ANYONE WITH MY COMMENTS.
4.) In 2 cases a piece broke off or was not working and WAS FIXED BY AMAZON OR THE MANUFACTURER, YET THEY STILL GIVE IT A 1 STAR, and to ME, that's JUST GOOD CUSTOMER SERVICE! ONE GUY SAID He LOVED IT AND ALREADY WAS USING IT!! But gave it a 1 Star because of the broken.

Should it have been broken? No. Could it have happened in transport? MOST LIKELY!!! Have you ever BEEN TO A DISTRIBUTION CENTER??? I HAVE (I used to work for one of the BIGGEST COMPANIES IN THE WORLD) and sometimes, some of the worker JUST THROW YOUR PRODUCTS TO EACH OTHER OR THROW THE PRODUCT ON PILES (Happens on the receiving end too)!

HERE are the comments I put to people in regard to their 1 star review (in a lot of cases I added something personal to that person's personal predicament... but you get the idea.

Work with Amazon instead of just complaining about the product and them. They would have returned in or replaced it... You JUST NEED TO GET ON CHAT OR PHONE, BUT NOT MY FIRST RECOMMENDATION (Maybe not after THIS amount of time...) but WOULD have before, Amazon is QUITE willing to work with you!)

PLEASE look at the comments OF ACTUAL INVESTIGATORS on this site ALL the comments from people who actually know what they are doing. No, YOU ARE NOT GOING TO GET THE SAME EQUIPMENT as GHOST ADVENTURES RIGHT OFF! They spend A LOT of money and time engineering and working with top scientist to gear their equipment. These types of investigations TAKE TIME, just be patient and TRY IT OUT AGAIN! It's worked for ME...

***************************************************************************************************************************************************
I HOPE I'VE HELPED NOT JUST YOU BUT OTHER PEOPLE READING THESE..... BE PATIENT, BE KIND, USE MULTIPLE TOOLS, DON'T GIVE UP AFTER THE FIRST TIME, RESEARCH (And use all the steps I've outlined) AREAS WITH HIGH ACTIVITY AND SEEK OUT OTHERS IN YOUR AREA WITH THE SAME INTERESTS!!!! This REALLY is a "TEAM" activity, not just for the safety, but for the fun, shared experiences and helping others and each other. Have fun and BE SAFE! THIS REALLY IS A GREAT TOOL FOR NEW AND EXPERIENCED USERS ALIKE. AGAIN, HOPE THIS HAS HELPED....

top_rant.text:
Update: still love it. All this week my neighbors Comcast kept going out, but my fire TV with DirecTV now works great on my measly 0-3mbps internet. I had some stuttering and thought I might have to upgrade my internet, but I just deleted the DirecTV now app and reloaded it (you'll need your password!) And it's been fantastic ever since! Very few glitches on live TV-way less than the Comcast set top box! My parents' Comcast tv is screwed up right now, channel not authorized every few minutes. Comcast tv is like landline phones: ditch it and save money and hassles. Comcast internet is great-if your neighborhood network of ok.

I canceled my Comcast TV subscription but kept my measly "up to 3Mbps" internet. I ordered this device and plugged it in. Clicked to confirm my Amazon account (came right up on screen) and selected "English". Then I navigated to the Direct TV Now app and selected it. It told me to sign up at direcTVnow.com/amazon...please note that there is only 1 "T" in the address.

I signed up for the free 7 day trail and swiped LEFT to go to the cheaper plan of $35/month. BAM: I'm channel surfing just like I had with Comcast...only now I have MORE channels!!!!

Why did I choose Direct TV Now over PlayStation Vue?
